rationale: Humanities at L3 is intentionally smaller than other categories — 25 leaves total — because the humanities papers in the summino corpus are the smallest L1 group and rich sub-splits would create thin leaves. Philosophy gets 6 leaves (analytic philosophy, ethics, philosophy of mind, philosophy of science, philosophy of language, social/political philosophy) because each is a recognized AOS in academic philosophy. History gets 5 leaves split by region/period. Archaeology gets 3 (science-based, classical, prehistoric).

subfields.name: Ethics & Moral Philosophy
subfields.tagline: Normative ethics, metaethics, applied ethics, bioethics.

subfields.name: Philosophy of Mind
subfields.tagline: Philosophy of mind, consciousness, mental content, embodied mind.

subfields.name: Philosophy of Science
subfields.tagline: Philosophy of science, philosophy of biology, philosophy of physics.

subfields.name: Philosophy of Language & Logic
subfields.tagline: Philosophy of language, philosophical logic, philosophy of math.

subfields.name: Metaphysics & Epistemology
subfields.tagline: Metaphysics, epistemology, modality, reasoning.

subfields.name: Political & Social Philosophy
subfields.tagline: Political philosophy, social philosophy, philosophy of law.

subfields.name: Ancient & Medieval History
subfields.tagline: Ancient history (Greek, Roman, Near East), medieval history.

subfields.name: Early Modern History
subfields.tagline: Early modern history (16th-18th c.), Atlantic world, scientific revolution history.

subfields.name: Modern & Contemporary History
subfields.tagline: 19th-21st century history, contemporary history.

subfields.name: Global & Comparative History
subfields.tagline: Global history, comparative history, world history.

subfields.name: Social, Cultural & Intellectual History
subfields.tagline: Social history, cultural history, intellectual history, environmental history.

subfields.name: History of Modern Science
subfields.tagline: History of modern science, history of physics, history of biology, history of computing.

subfields.name: History of Medicine & Technology
subfields.tagline: History of medicine, history of technology, history of mathematics.

subfields.name: Science in Colonial & Global Contexts
subfields.tagline: Science in colonial contexts, non-Western science history, global science.

subfields.name: Archaeological Science (Radiocarbon, Isotopes)
subfields.tagline: Archaeological science — radiocarbon, isotopes, archaeo-genetics, scientific dating.

subfields.name: Classical & Mediterranean Archaeology
subfields.tagline: Classical archaeology, Mediterranean antiquity archaeology, Roman/Greek material culture.

subfields.name: Prehistoric & Historic Archaeology
subfields.tagline: Prehistoric archaeology, historic archaeology, indigenous archaeology, heritage management.

subfields.name: Greek & Latin Philology
subfields.tagline: Greek and Latin philology, classical reception studies.

subfields.name: Ancient Near East & Mediterranean Studies
subfields.tagline: Ancient Near East studies, Egyptology, ancient Mediterranean studies.

subfields.name: Modern & Comparative Literature
subfields.tagline: Modern literature, world literature, comparative literature, postcolonial literature.

subfields.name: Literary Theory & Criticism
subfields.tagline: Literary criticism, literary theory, narrative theory.

subfields.name: Comparative Religion & Theology
subfields.tagline: Comparative religion, theology, religious history.

subfields.name: Art History Periods & Museum Studies
subfields.tagline: Art history periods, museum studies, history of architecture, visual culture analysis.

subfields.name: Musicology & Music Theory
subfields.tagline: Musicology, music theory, ethnomusicology.

subfields.name: Theatre, Dance & Film Studies
subfields.tagline: Theatre studies, dance studies, film studies as humanities.

uncovered_concerns: Digital humanities L2 has one big leaf only — computational text analysis fold there at L3.
Did not split history-by-region (e.g. African history, Asian history) — folds under global/comparative history.
